user_query:
I need a safe, repeatable AdsPower setup plan for our outreach team. Please read the following files under input/ and use them to generate a deterministic command sequence without executing anything:
- input/groups.yaml — target groups to create with remarks
- input/proxies.csv — proxies to create (type,host,port)
- input/profiles.json — browser profiles to create (name/platform/target_group_name/proxy preference/fingerprint hints)
- input/port.txt — if present, use this port in commands via a --port flag where applicable

Deliverables (write all outputs under output/):
1) output/plan.md — a detailed, step-by-step plan with prerequisites, assumptions, safety guardrails, and an ordered workflow. Include: how to verify the CLI package source link, when to prompt for API_KEY (headless only), why to run check-status before and after, and how to retrieve group IDs before creating browsers. Add an Assumptions section and a Rollback section.
2) output/commands.txt — the exact adspower-browser commands to: check API status; list/create groups as needed; create proxies; create all requested browser profiles with JSON args (including at least one fingerprintConfig example); open at least two profiles; and show opened browsers. Do not include any privileged commands or install scripts. Use the port from input/port.txt when present.
3) output/summary.json — a machine-readable summary that includes total counts for groups_to_create, proxies_to_create, profiles_to_create, profiles_to_open, and a command_counts object with counts of create-group, create-proxy, create-browser, and open-browser lines present in output/commands.txt.
4) output/rollback.txt — commands to safely close all profiles and clean up created assets (e.g., delete created profiles and/or proxies), plus any cache-clear instructions if required.

Important:
- Do not run anything — just produce the plan and command text.
- Use only adspower-browser commands and JSON arguments where required.
- Follow safety guardrails: verify the npm package page before first use, never fabricate or store API_KEY, and never use sudo/curl/dpkg/Invoke-WebRequest/Start-Process in your command files.
